## Purpose
Accept a Figma file URL or exported JSON in the UX Planning stage. Extract screens, components, design tokens, and interaction annotations. Output a structured screen list + per-screen state spec.

## Skill ID
`3b768b92-e4b2-47f1-9172-3bc20eeb7695`

## Trigger
- Product brief or PRD includes a Figma file URL.
- UX Architect or product-manager explicitly calls the skill on a UX Planning issue.

## Required Input
- Figma file URL (public share link) or exported Figma JSON.
- Approved PRD with permission matrix.

## Steps (summary)
1. Fetch Figma file via REST API v1 or parse exported JSON.
2. Identify top-level FRAME nodes as screens; skip component/design pages.
3. Extract: screen name, components, prototype interactions, design tokens.
4. Map screens to user roles from PRD permission matrix.
5. Generate per-screen spec: route, role visibility, components, states (empty/loading/error), mobile/RTL notes.
6. Identify gaps: PRD-only and Figma-only screens.
7. Post spec comment + attach `ux-spec.md` to UX Planning issue.

## Output
- Sitemap from Figma frames
- Screen list with role visibility + state matrix
- Design token extract (colors, text, effects)
- Gap list (PRD vs Figma)

## Quality Checklist
- [ ] All top-level Figma frames mapped (component pages excluded)
- [ ] Role visibility sourced from PRD matrix only
- [ ] Empty/loading/error states noted per screen (or flagged missing)
- [ ] Design token extract complete
- [ ] Gap list populated
- [ ] Figma API version pinned to v1
- [ ] No credentials in output

## Failure Handling
- Private/expired URL: prompt for exported JSON; set issue blocked.
- Rate limit: retry after 30s; fall back to exported JSON.
- Missing PRD matrix: flag all screens as unassigned; request from product-manager.
- No interactions: note and continue.

## Improvement Rule
After 3 uses: compare spec completeness vs baseline. If <80%, improve frame-to-screen heuristics.
After 5 uses: run `cross-project-pattern-extractor` to codify recurring gap types.

## Source Issue
DEV-84 (Research Intelligence benchmark extraction)

## Definition of Done
- [x] Skill registered in workspace catalog
- [x] Skill spec filed here in 08 - Skill Library
- [x] Validation test confirms skill steps produce correct output format
issues.title: SKILL: figma-to-ux-spec ŌĆö translate Figma file into screen list + state specs

## Skill: parallel-issue-dispatcher

**Skill ID:** `9f4b229b-4f75-454b-9548-2df4e4b80916`
**Source:** DEV-82 ŌĆö extracted from Cursor "Build in Parallel" + Replit Agent 4 parallel task execution benchmarks (DEV-77, DEV-78).

## Purpose
Auto-identify independent sub-issues within a parent and batch-promote all of them to `--status todo` simultaneously, using a dependency DAG to determine readiness. Eliminates the one-by-one sprint-commander bottleneck.

## Trigger
- Any parent issue with 3+ sub-issues in `backlog` status where at least some are independent.
- Explicitly invoked by sprint-commander or ceo after a parent issue's build plan is finalized.

## Required Input
- `parent_issue_id` ŌĆö the parent issue whose sub-issues to process.
- Sub-issues must have a `Dependencies` field listing referenced issue IDs or `none`.

## Steps
1. Fetch sub-issues via `multica issue list --parent <id> --output json`.
2. Parse each sub-issue's `Dependencies` field; treat missing as independent.
3. Build a DAG; mark sub-issues *ready* when all declared dependencies are `done` or `in_review`.
4. Batch-promote all ready sub-issues to `todo`.
5. Post dispatch-map comment on parent (promoted vs. blocked + reasons).
6. Re-run on each sub-issue `done` event to promote newly unblocked issues.

## Output
- All independent sub-issues promoted to `todo` in one pass.
- Dispatch-map comment on parent issue.

## Quality Checklist
- [ ] DAG correctly identifies all independent vs. blocked issues
- [ ] No issue promoted if any upstream dependency is not `done` or `in_review`
- [ ] Missing `Dependencies` field treated as independent (not an error)
- [ ] Parent comment posted with full dispatch map
- [ ] Re-run logic triggers on sub-issue `done` transitions
- [ ] No sub-issue promoted more than once per dispatch cycle

## Failure Handling
- CLI failure on fetch ŌåÆ post `blocked` comment, stop.
- Unparseable description ŌåÆ log as "unparseable ŌĆö treated as independent", promote.
- Single promotion failure ŌåÆ log and continue batch; don't abort.

## Improvement Rule
After 3 uses: measure parallelization rate (independent/total). If < 50%, audit issue-creation practices.
After 5 uses: refine or retire.

## Definition of Done
- [x] Skill spec complete with all required fields
- [x] Registered via `multica skill create`
- [ ] Assigned to sprint-commander agent
- [ ] Tested against a real parent with mixed independent + blocked sub-issues
issues.title: SKILL: parallel-issue-dispatcher

Source: [getsentry/sentry-mcp](https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-mcp)

## Agents to Wire

| Agent | Token Type | Tools Allowed |
|-------|-----------|---------------|
| deployment-engineer | Read + write (assign/resolve issues) | Full Sentry MCP |
| qa-engineer | Read-only | Error events, issue list |
| security-auditor | Read-only | Error events, stack traces, release health |

- `mcp_config` added to all 3 agents
- Sentry project scoped to workspace's deployed apps only (not all Sentry orgs)
- Smoke test: deployment-engineer can list recent errors for a test project

- [ ] 3 agents wired
- [ ] Token permissions match table above
- [ ] Sentry project scoping confirmed
- [ ] Smoke test passes

- DEV-89 (security review) must be DONE before promotion to `todo`

### Candidates to deep-dive:

1. **GitHub MCP** ŌĆö [github/github-mcp-server](https://github.com/github/github-mcp-server)
2. **Supabase MCP** ŌĆö [supabase-community/supabase-mcp](https://github.com/supabase-community/supabase-mcp)
3. **Playwright MCP** ŌĆö [microsoft/playwright-mcp](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp)
4. **Context7 MCP** ŌĆö [upstash/context7](https://github.com/upstash/context7)
5. **Sentry MCP** ŌĆö [getsentry/sentry-mcp](https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-mcp)

Additionally, please verify + locate GitHub URLs for three candidates flagged as "URL pending verification" in DEV-86:
- **Exa MCP** ŌĆö Exa semantic search (exa.ai)
- **Resend MCP** ŌĆö Transactional email
- **Brave Search MCP** ŌĆö confirm whether an actively-maintained community fork exists post-archive

## Expected Output

Per each of the top-5 candidates:
- **License**: confirm OSI-approved (Apache 2, MIT, etc.)
- **Maintainer activity**: last commit date, open issues count, release cadence
- **Security posture**: any known CVEs, supply chain risk (dependency count, lockfile present), permissions requested vs what is actually needed
- **Verdict**: APPROVE / APPROVE WITH CONDITIONS / REJECT ŌĆö with a one-line reason

For the 3 "URL pending" candidates: provide the confirmed GitHub URL + brief health check (stars, last commit, license).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 top candidates reviewed with approve/reject verdicts
- [ ] 3 pending URLs resolved
- [ ] Results posted as a comment on this issue

## Dependencies
- DEV-86 (inventory) ŌĆö already done
- Wiring sub-issues DEV-87 through DEV-91 are blocked on this review

## Risk
github-analyst may find a security issue that changes the import decision for one of the top-5. If that happens: post a comment on DEV-61 and the relevant wiring sub-issue before rejecting.

## Definition of Done
All 5 verdicts posted. Pending URLs resolved. Wiring sub-issues can be promoted.

# Top-20 MCP Server Inventory ŌĆö devosnt App Factory

**Prepared by:** research-scout ┬Ę **Date:** 2026-05-26  
**Parent:** DEV-61 ┬Ę **Stack baseline:** Next.js + Supabase + Vercel

---

## Methodology

Surveyed 30 candidates across official registries, Anthropic/MCP reference servers, and first-party vendor implementations. Filtered against App Factory build categories (CRM, HR, delivery, booking, SaaS, dashboards, mobile) and current stack. Eliminated redundant/secondary options. 20 servers made the cut; 10 deferred or rejected with reasons.

---

## Top-20 Inventory

| # | Server | GitHub URL | Purpose | Maintainer | Perms / Tools Exposed | Risk | Recommended Agents | Decision |
|---|--------|-----------|---------|-----------|----------------------|------|-------------------|---------|
| 1 | **GitHub MCP** | [github/github-mcp-server](https://github.com/github/github-mcp-server) | Repo management, issues, PRs, code review, branch ops | GitHub (official) | Full GitHub API (read + write); scoped to PAT or GitHub App token | **Medium** ŌĆö token scope is critical; restrict to specific repos | claude-code-lead-developer, frontend-engineer, backend-engineer, full-stack-engineer, mobile-engineer, qa-engineer, github-analyst | **IMPORT** |
| 2 | **Supabase MCP** | [supabase-community/supabase-mcp](https://github.com/supabase-community/supabase-mcp) | DB queries, migrations, edge functions, auth, storage ŌĆö full Supabase project access | Supabase Community | 20+ tools: SQL, schema, migrations, auth admin, Storage; `read_only` flag available | **High** ŌĆö management API can run migrations; use project-scoped PAT + `read_only` for non-architect agents | database-architect, backend-engineer, full-stack-engineer | **IMPORT** |
| 3 | **Playwright MCP** | [microsoft/playwright-mcp](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright-mcp) | Browser automation, E2E testing, visual regression, web scraping | Microsoft (official) | Browser control, screenshots, form fill, network intercept; sandboxed browser context | **Medium** ŌĆö autonomous browser execution; sandbox + network policy recommended | qa-engineer, bug-hunter | **IMPORT** |
| 4 | **Context7 MCP** | [upstash/context7](https://github.com/upstash/context7) | Injects version-specific library docs into prompts; prevents hallucinated API calls | Upstash | 2 tools: `resolve-library-id`, `get-library-docs`; read-only external API call | **Low** ŌĆö read-only, no data egress; requires CONTEXT7_API_KEY | frontend-engineer, backend-engineer, full-stack-engineer, mobile-engineer, claude-code-lead-developer | **IMPORT** |
| 5 | **Sentry MCP** | [getsentry/sentry-mcp](https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-mcp) | Error tracking, stack traces, performance telemetry for post-launch monitoring | Sentry (official) | Read error events, releases, issues; write: assign/resolve issues | **Medium** ŌĆö exposes raw stack traces + user context; restrict to read for most agents | deployment-engineer, qa-engineer, security-auditor | **IMPORT** |
| 6 | **Stripe MCP** | [stripe/ai](https://github.com/stripe/ai) | Payments, subscriptions, invoices, customers ŌĆö 25 tools across 13 resource types | Stripe (official) | Full Stripe API; remote hosted at mcp.stripe.com with OAuth | **High** ŌĆö financial data; wire test-mode key only; prod key requires explicit approval gate | integration-architect, backend-engineer | **IMPORT** |
| 7 | **Vercel MCP** | [vercel/vercel-mcp-overview](https://github.com/vercel/vercel-mcp-overview) | Deployment status, rollback, env var management, preview URL inspection | Vercel (official) | Deploy/manage projects, list deployments, read env vars; OAuth | **Medium** ŌĆö can trigger deploys; deployment-engineer should gate prod actions | deployment-engineer, environment-manager, release-manager | **IMPORT** |
| 8 | **Figma MCP** | [figma/mcp-server-guide](https://github.com/figma/mcp-server-guide) | Design specs ŌåÆ code; pull design tokens, layers, annotations; write-to-canvas (beta) | Figma (official, open beta) | Read design files; write-to-canvas; hosted at mcp.figma.com | **Low** ŌĆö read-only in default config; write-to-canvas gated by Figma auth | ui-designer, ux-architect, frontend-engineer | **IMPORT** |
| 9 | **Cloudflare MCP** | [cloudflare/mcp-server-cloudflare](https://github.com/cloudflare/mcp-server-cloudflare) | Workers, KV, R2, D1, DNS, security rules ŌĆö Cloudflare account management | Cloudflare (official) | Broad CF account access; domain-specific sub-servers available | **High** ŌĆö can modify DNS + firewall rules; scope to specific zones; deployment-engineer gate | deployment-engineer, solution-architect | **IMPORT** |
| 10 | **Memory MCP** | [modelcontextprotocol/servers](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers) | Knowledge graph persistent memory (entities + relations) per agent | Anthropic / MCP steering (official) | Local file system read/write only; no external calls | **Low** ŌĆö local only; choose storage path carefully | ceo, solution-architect, memory-curator, product-manager | **IMPORT** |
| 11 | **Sequential Thinking MCP** | [modelcontextprotocol/servers](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers) | Dynamic multi-step reasoning; structured thought chains | Anthropic / MCP steering (official) | No external access; pure computation | **Low** ŌĆö stateless, no data egress | ceo, product-manager, api-architect, solution-architect | **IMPORT** |
| 12 | **Fetch MCP** | [modelcontextprotocol/servers](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers) | Fetch + convert web pages / docs into LLM-ready markdown | Anthropic / MCP steering (official) | External HTTP GET; URL-based; no auth | **Low** ŌĆö outbound HTTP; SSRF risk if user-supplied URLs not validated | research-scout, docs-analyst, github-analyst | **IMPORT** |
| 13 | **Git MCP** | [modelcontextprotocol/servers](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers) | Local git repo operations: read history, diff, search, commits | Anthropic / MCP steering (official) | Local filesystem git ops; read + write local repo | **Low** ŌĆö local only; no remote push in default config | frontend-engineer, backend-engineer, full-stack-engineer, mobile-engineer | **IMPORT** |

| 15 | **PostgreSQL MCP** | [crystaldba/postgres-mcp](https://github.com/crystaldba/postgres-mcp) | Schema analysis, query tuning, direct SQL for DB introspection | crystaldba (community, active) | Direct Postgres connection; read + write; connection string required | **High** ŌĆö direct DB access; use read-only DB user for non-architect agents | database-architect, backend-engineer | **IMPORT (read-only user)** |
| 16 | **Docker Hub MCP** | [docker/hub-mcp](https://github.com/docker/hub-mcp) | Container image search, repository management, Docker Hub API | Docker (official) | Docker Hub API; image pull, push, repo management | **Medium** ŌĆö can push images; scope to specific org | deployment-engineer, environment-manager | **IMPORT** |
| 17 | **Brave Search MCP** | [modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived) | Privacy-first web search; no tracking | Anthropic ref (archived ŌĆö community forks active) | Web search API; requires BRAVE_API_KEY | **Low** ŌĆö read-only; note: archived, use community fork or substitute Tavily | research-scout | **IMPORT (verify active fork ŌĆö github-analyst to confirm)** |
| 18 | **Exa MCP** | URL to be verified by github-analyst | Semantic neural web + code search; ideal for prior-art research | Exa (official) | Web + code search API; requires EXA_API_KEY | **Low** ŌĆö read-only search | research-scout, github-analyst | **IMPORT (URL pending verification)** |
| 19 | **Google Maps MCP** | [modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived) | Location, routing, distance ŌĆö delivery + booking apps | Google / Anthropic ref (archived) | Maps API read; GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY required | **Low** ŌĆö read-only geo data | integration-architect | **IMPORT (archived ŌĆö verify maintained fork)** |
| 20 | **Resend MCP** | URL to be verified by github-analyst | Transactional email sending for SaaS + notification flows | Resend (official or community) | Email send API; RESEND_API_KEY; From address required | **Medium** ŌĆö email send; restrict to verified domain; rate-limit | integration-architect, backend-engineer | **IMPORT (URL pending verification)** |

---

## Rejected / Deferred (10)

| Server | Reason |
|--------|--------|
| AWS MCP (awslabs/mcp) | Stack is Vercel + Supabase; AWS is secondary. Revisit if multi-cloud. |
| MongoDB MCP | Stack uses PostgreSQL exclusively. Redundant. |
| Linear MCP | Workspace uses Multica, not Linear. |
| Discord MCP | Not in current stack; Slack preferred if comms MCP ever needed. |
| Slack MCP | Nice-to-have for team comms automation, but not on critical path now. Defer to Q3. |
| Notion MCP | Not in stack. If knowledge base need emerges, revisit. |
| Terraform MCP (HashiCorp) | IaC needs currently covered by Vercel + Cloudflare native tools. Defer. |
| dbt MCP | Analytics semantic layer; relevant only post-scale, not in build phase. |
| Filesystem MCP | Redundant ŌĆö Claude Code runtime already provides local file ops natively. |
| Vantage MCP (cloud cost) | Useful post-launch for cost visibility. Defer until first production apps are live. |

---

## Top-5 Import Priority + Wiring Plan

| Priority | Server | Primary Agents to Wire | Risk Mitigation | Wiring Steps |
|----------|--------|----------------------|-----------------|-------------|
| **P1** | GitHub MCP | claude-code-lead-developer, frontend-engineer, backend-engineer, full-stack-engineer, mobile-engineer, qa-engineer, github-analyst | Scope PAT to workspace repos only; read-only for QA agents | `multica agent update <id> --mcp-config ...` with token scoped per agent |
| **P2** | Supabase MCP | database-architect, backend-engineer, full-stack-engineer | `project_ref` scoped per app; `read_only: true` for engineer agents; management-level only for database-architect | Set per-agent `mcp_config` with separate tokens per role |
| **P3** | Playwright MCP | qa-engineer, bug-hunter | Run in sandboxed browser; no credential storage in browser session | Add to qa-engineer + bug-hunter `mcp_config`; network policy: block internal endpoints |
| **P4** | Context7 MCP | claude-code-lead-developer, frontend-engineer, backend-engineer, full-stack-engineer, mobile-engineer | Read-only; no sensitive data; API key from Upstash | Wire `CONTEXT7_API_KEY` via environment-manager; add to all code agent `mcp_config` |
| **P5** | Sentry MCP | deployment-engineer, qa-engineer, security-auditor | Read-only token for qa + security; write token only for deployment-engineer | Provision Sentry auth token per agent role |

---

## Sources

## Input
Pattern imported from benchmark sweep: Bolt.new Figma import (drop Figma designs into chat ŌåÆ working app with visual reference).
Source brief: [DEV-76](mention://issue/ee26fb58-8f56-41fc-9543-ca2f5c230f85) (Bolt.new)

## Skill to Build: `figma-to-ux-spec`

### Purpose
Accept a Figma file URL or exported JSON/ZIP in the UX Planning stage. Extract screens, components, design tokens, and interaction annotations. Output a structured screen list + per-screen state spec that the UX Architect currently writes manually ŌĆö reducing UX Planning time for design-led projects.

### Trigger
- A product brief or PRD includes a Figma file URL.
- UX Architect or product-manager explicitly calls the skill on a UX Planning issue.

### Required Input
- Figma file URL (public share link) or exported Figma JSON
- Product brief / PRD (for context mapping)

### Steps
1. Fetch Figma file via Figma REST API (or parse exported JSON).
2. Identify top-level frames as screens. Extract:
   - Screen name
   - Components present (buttons, forms, tables, navigation)
   - Design tokens (color, typography, spacing)
   - Annotations / prototype interactions
3. Map screens to user roles from the PRD permission matrix.
4. Generate per-screen spec: screen name, route, role visibility, components, states (empty/loading/error), interactions.
5. Output as structured markdown (compatible with UX Architect's screen list format).
6. Post output as comment on the UX Planning issue + attach as file.

### Output
- Sitemap derived from Figma frames
- Screen list with role visibility and state matrix
- Design token extract (colors, fonts, spacing)
- Any gaps identified (screens in PRD missing from Figma, or vice versa)

### Quality Checklist
- [ ] All top-level Figma frames mapped to screens
- [ ] Role visibility sourced from PRD permission matrix, not inferred
- [ ] Empty/loading/error states explicitly noted (or flagged as missing in Figma)
- [ ] Design token extract complete
- [ ] Gaps between PRD and Figma listed

### Failure Handling
If Figma URL is inaccessible (private/expired): prompt user for exported JSON or Figma export file. Do not skip ŌĆö missing design is a blocker.

### Improvement Rule
After 3 uses, compare spec completeness (manual UX Architect review score) to baseline. If < 80% match, improve frame-to-screen heuristics.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Skill spec written and filed in `08 - Skill Library`
- [ ] skill-builder implements skill
- [ ] Test: run against a real Figma public file and verify output matches manual UX spec format

## Dependencies
- [DEV-62](mention://issue/c289d731-eaf9-4747-814f-c122f1ed08cd) benchmark rollup (this parent)
- Figma API access (public share links should work without auth; private files need FIGMA_TOKEN env var)

## Risk
Low-Medium. Read-only Figma access. Risk: Figma API schema changes. Mitigation: pin Figma API version.

## Definition of Done
Skill filed in 08 - Skill Library. Tested with a real Figma file. No secrets committed.

## Input
Pattern imported from benchmark sweep: Replit Agent 3/4 autonomous test-fix loop (3├Ś faster, 10├Ś cheaper than Computer Use per Replit's benchmarks).
Source brief: [DEV-78](mention://issue/74a6652c-9293-45ca-b6f6-9ff0e2459a58) (Replit Agent)

## Skill to Build: `autonomous-test-fix-loop`

### Purpose
After a build agent ships a feature and marks it `in_review`, automatically run the QA test plan against the live preview URL, detect failures, and create a self-healing fix sub-issue assigned back to the build agent. Human only reviews when the loop converges or after a configurable max-retry limit.

### Trigger
- A build sub-issue transitions to `in_review`.
- A QA engineer has attached a test plan to the issue.

### Required Input
- Issue ID of the feature being tested
- Preview URL (from issue metadata `deploy_url` or comment)
- QA test plan (from issue description or QA sub-issue)

### Steps
1. Read the QA test plan from the issue.
2. Execute each test case against the preview URL (headless browser / Playwright).
3. If all pass: comment "All tests passed. Ready for QA review." ŌĆö done.
4. If any fail: create a fix sub-issue (`--status todo`, assigned to original build agent) with the failure log.
5. On fix sub-issue `in_review`: re-run the loop from step 2.
6. After 3 failed retry cycles: escalate to qa-engineer and ceo with full failure log.

### Output
- Pass/fail result comment on the feature issue.
- Fix sub-issue (if failures found).
- Escalation comment after 3 retry failures.

### Quality Checklist
- [ ] Test runner executes all test cases from the QA plan
- [ ] Failure log captures exact assertion + screenshot
- [ ] Fix sub-issue created with correct parent link and build agent assignee
- [ ] Max-retry cap enforced (3 cycles)
- [ ] Escalation posted after cap reached

### Failure Handling
If preview URL is unreachable: post `blocked` comment with reason. Do not create fix sub-issue.

### Improvement Rule
After 5 uses, measure mean cycles-to-green. If > 2, audit QA test plan quality with qa-engineer.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Skill spec written and filed in `08 - Skill Library`
- [ ] skill-builder implements skill
- [ ] Integration test: one build issue with a known failing test ŌåÆ fix loop fires and converges

## Dependencies
- [DEV-62](mention://issue/c289d731-eaf9-4747-814f-c122f1ed08cd) benchmark rollup (this parent)

## Risk
Medium. Creates sub-issues and transitions statuses autonomously ŌĆö test the retry cap carefully to avoid loops.

## Definition of Done
Skill filed in 08 - Skill Library. Tested. No secrets.

## Input
Pattern imported from benchmark sweep: Cursor "Build in Parallel" + Replit Agent 4 parallel task execution.
Source briefs: [DEV-77](mention://issue/e5cf1d27-3c5b-458a-967b-95b4e3f6b017) (Cursor), [DEV-78](mention://issue/74a6652c-9293-45ca-b6f6-9ff0e2459a58) (Replit Agent)

## Skill to Build: `parallel-issue-dispatcher`

### Purpose
Auto-identify independent sub-issues within a parent and batch-promote all of them to `--status todo` simultaneously. Today, the sprint-commander promotes sub-issues one-by-one; this skill reads the dependency graph and launches all non-blocked work in parallel.

### Trigger
- Any parent issue with 3+ sub-issues in `backlog` status where some are independent.
- Explicitly called by sprint-commander or ceo after a parent issue's build plan is finalized.

### Required Input
- Parent issue ID
- List of sub-issues with their declared `Dependencies` field values

### Steps
1. Read all sub-issues of parent. Parse each issue's `Dependencies` field.
2. Build a dependency DAG: which sub-issues have no upstream dependencies (or all upstream are `done`/`in_review`)?
3. Promote all dependency-free sub-issues to `--status todo` in a single batch.
4. Comment on parent: "Dispatched N sub-issues in parallel: [list]. Remaining M are backlog pending: [list]."
5. When any sub-issue reaches `done`, re-run the DAG and promote newly unblocked issues.

### Output
- All independent sub-issues promoted to `todo` in one pass.
- Parent comment with dispatch map.

### Quality Checklist
- [ ] DAG correctly identifies all independent vs. blocked issues
- [ ] No issue promoted if any upstream dependency is not `done` or `in_review`
- [ ] Parent comment posted with full dispatch map
- [ ] Re-run logic triggers on sub-issue completion

### Failure Handling
If a sub-issue has no `Dependencies` field, treat it as independent (safe default).

### Improvement Rule
After 3 uses, measure actual parallelization rate (independent/total). If < 50%, audit issue creation practices.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Skill spec written and filed in `08 - Skill Library`
- [ ] skill-builder implements skill with all required fields
- [ ] Tested against a real parent issue with mixed independent + blocked sub-issues

## Dependencies
- [DEV-62](mention://issue/c289d731-eaf9-4747-814f-c122f1ed08cd) benchmark rollup (this parent)

## Risk
Low. Read-only analysis + status promotion ŌĆö fully reversible.

## Definition of Done
Skill filed in 08 - Skill Library. Tested. No secrets.

## What It Does

Replit Agent is a cloud-native, full-stack AI software development agent. Describe what you want in natural language; Agent plans, writes, tests, debugs, and deploys the app ŌĆö autonomously, in a browser, with no local setup. Supports web apps, mobile apps, APIs, automations, slides, and static sites.

---

## Timeline of Major Releases (2025ŌĆō2026)

| Date | Release | Headline |
|------|---------|----------|
| Feb 2025 | Agent v2 | Powered by Claude 3.7 Sonnet; faster, more accurate; free-to-try checkpoints |
| Jul 2025 | Web Search | Real-time internet access eliminates knowledge cutoffs |
| Aug 2025 | App Storage | Auto-generates backend + frontend storage code |

| Oct 2025 | Connectors Platform | 30+ integrations: Stripe, Figma, Notion, Salesforce, Slack, Telegram, email |
| Nov 2025 | Design Mode | Interactive designs / static sites in under 2 min; Figma import |
| Dec 2025 | Custom MCP Servers | Connect to remote MCP servers; hundreds of external tools |
| Early 2026 | Agent 4 | Parallel artifact builds; Design Canvas (infinite board); shared project collaboration; plan-while-build |
| Apr 2026 | Library & CVE auto-patch | Stock/AI images in Library sidebar; auto-scans deps against new CVEs; auto-prepares patches |

---

## Current State (Agent 4, May 2026)

**Autonomous run length:** Up to 200 minutes uninterrupted.

**What it builds:**
- Web apps, APIs, full-stack mobile apps (with backend, DB, storage)
- Automated agents (Slack/email/Telegram bots, scheduled jobs)
- Presentations and static sites

**Backend / Database:**
- Built-in Replit Database (key-value)
- App Storage for file/blob assets
- External DB via connectors (Supabase, Postgres, etc.)
- Full-stack mobile with AI integrations

**Deployment:**
- One-click deploy via Replit Deploy ŌĆö live URL in seconds, no DevOps needed
- Autoscale (pay-per-request, from $1/mo), Reserved VM (from $20/mo), Scheduled, Static hosting (free)
- Custom domains supported
- Enterprise: Security Center with CVE detection, SBOM export, auto-patch preparation

**Collaboration (Agent 4):**
- Shared project; each teammate has own Agent chat thread
- Kanban board: Drafts ŌåÆ Active ŌåÆ Ready ŌåÆ Done
- Agent-assisted conflict resolution (replaces old fork-and-merge)
- Plan-while-build: start new planning while current build runs

**AI Models:**
- Standard: Claude Sonnet-class models
- Power mode: Claude Opus 4.7 (Anthropic)
- Live web search for real-time data

**Connectors:**
- 30+ native: Stripe, Notion, Figma, Salesforce, Linear, Slack, Telegram, email
- Custom MCP server support for hundreds more tools

---

## Replit Agent vs. Ghostwriter (Legacy)

| Dimension | Ghostwriter (2022ŌĆō2023) | Replit Agent (2024ŌĆōpresent) |
|-----------|------------------------|-----------------------------|
| Scope | Inline code completion & suggestions | End-to-end project build, test, deploy |
| Interaction | Editor companion (autocomplete, explain) | Conversational: describe ŌåÆ Agent executes |
| File coverage | Single-file suggestions | Multi-file, full project |
| Deployment | Manual | One-click, automated |
| Autonomy | None ŌĆö developer drives | Up to 200 min unsupervised |
| Status | Rebranded to "Replit Assistant" in 2023 | Core platform product today |

Ghostwriter was retired as a brand in Oct 2023. Replit Agent is now the primary product.

---

## Pricing (2026)

| Plan | Price | Agent Credits | Notes |
|------|-------|--------------|-------|
| Starter (Free) | $0 | Limited trial | Up to 10 dev apps; 1 vCPU / 2 GiB RAM |
| Core | $17/mo (annual) | $20/mo credits | Full Agent access; unlimited app publishing |
| Teams | $35ŌĆō$40/user/mo | $40/mo per user | Shared workspace |
| Pro | $95/mo (annual) | Shared pool | Up to 15 builders; no per-seat fees |

Effort-based pricing (Jun 2025): simple changes ~$0.25/checkpoint; complex tasks bundled into one higher-cost checkpoint. Deployment costs extra.

---

## Key Strengths

1. **Zero local setup** ŌĆö fully browser-based; works for non-developers ("vibe coding")
2. **Longest autonomous run** ŌĆö 200 min uninterrupted, self-testing in real browser
3. **Connectors breadth** ŌĆö 30+ native + unlimited via MCP
4. **Fastest deploy** ŌĆö no DevOps, live URL in seconds
5. **Agent-spawning** ŌĆö can create scheduled automation agents (Slack bots, cron jobs)
6. **Agent 4 parallelism** ŌĆö multiple artifacts/tasks build simultaneously

## Notable Limits

1. **Vendor lock-in** ŌĆö cloud-only; migrating complex apps out is non-trivial
2. **Pricing unpredictability** ŌĆö effort-based credits make large build costs unclear upfront
3. **Not repo-native** ŌĆö weaker for teams with existing local Git workflows
4. **Complex enterprise features** ŌĆö CVE patching, SBOM only in Enterprise (pricing unlisted)
5. **Power mode cost** ŌĆö Claude Opus 4.7 burns credits fast

## Vendor: Cursor (cursor.sh) ŌĆö Agent Mode Brief
**Date:** 2026-05-26 | **Analyst:** docs-analyst | **Parent:** DEV-62

---

## 1. What Agent Mode Does Autonomously

**Core capabilities:**
- **Multi-file edits in one pass** ŌĆö understands file relationships; adding an API endpoint automatically updates routes, types, and tests
- **Terminal command execution** ŌĆö runs shell commands, installs packages, executes tests inside the working environment
- **Web browsing** ŌĆö generates search queries, browses docs/Stack Overflow/API references to resolve unknowns at runtime
- **Computer Use (since Feb 24, 2026)** ŌĆö cloud agents get a full desktop+browser VM; they open browsers, navigate to localhost, click through UI to visually verify their own code changes
- **Background/Cloud Agents** ŌĆö async cloud VMs with ephemeral repo checkouts; takes a GitHub issue or Slack message ŌåÆ produces a draft PR without the developer's machine being open
- **Multi-repo parallel execution** ŌĆö Agents Window surfaces all local + cloud agents in sidebar; launch agents across mobile, web, desktop, Slack, GitHub, Linear simultaneously
- **Multitask/async subagents** (`/multitask`, added Apr 24, 2026) ŌĆö spawns isolated subagents per independent task branch instead of queuing serially
- **BugBot** ŌĆö automated PR reviewer; by Feb 2026 upgraded to auto-fixer with ~80% resolution rate

---

## 2. Recent Major Features / Changelog (2025ŌĆō2026)

| Date | Version | Key Change |
|------|---------|-----------|
| Oct 2025 | 2.0 | Composer model launched (4x faster, <30s per turn); Background Agents GA; Memories (persistent project context); Jupyter support; one-click MCP |
| Mar 2026 | 3.0 | Full rebuild from scratch, agent-first interface; Agents Window (parallel agent mgmt); Cursor Marketplace (plugins); cloudŌåölocal handoff; multi-repo; Design Mode (annotate browser UI for agents) |
| Apr 24, 2026 | 3.x | `/multitask` async subagents; 35% of Cursor's own PRs now authored by cloud agents |
| May 13, 2026 | 3.4 | Full-screen tabs for files/canvases/PRs/terminals; compact chat response density controls |
| May 18, 2026 | ŌĆö | Composer 2.5 (better long-running task endurance, instruction-following) |
| May 19, 2026 | ŌĆö | Jira integration ŌĆö assign to @Cursor or mention in comments; agent delivers PR link |
| May 20, 2026 | 3.5 | Shared Canvases (shareable live agent artifacts); Automations multi-repo; non-code automations (Slack digests, finance tracking) |

---

## 3. Pricing Tiers (as of May 2026)

| Plan | Price | Key Limits |
|------|-------|-----------|
| Hobby | Free | Limited agent requests, limited Tab completions |
| Pro | $20/mo | Unlimited Tab completions, standard agent request pool, $20 model credit |
| Pro+ | $60/mo | 3x credit pool vs Pro |
| Ultra | $200/mo | 20x usage multiplier, priority feature access |
| Teams | $40/user/mo | Everything in Pro + shared rules/chats, SSO/SAML, RBAC, centralized billing |
| Enterprise | Custom | Custom SLA, security controls |

Composer 2.5 model token pricing: Standard $0.50/$2.50 per M in/out; Fast (default) $3.00/$15.00 per M in/out.

---

## 4. Plugin / Extensions Ecosystem

**Cursor Marketplace** (launched Mar 2026):
- Consolidates MCPs, skills, subagents, rules, and hooks into a single in-IDE surface
- One-click install; private team marketplace option for Enterprise
- Notable integrations: Amplitude, AWS, Figma, Linear, Stripe, Jira (native), Slack (native), GitHub (native)
- VS Code extensions still fully supported (it's a VS Code fork)
- 30+ new plugins added in first wave post-launch

---

## 5. Key Strengths

1. **Tab completions** ŌĆö proprietary specialized model; real-time inline prediction; no comparable CLI tool has this
2. **Visual inline diffs** ŌĆö line-level change review, staged approval; superior UX for humans reviewing agent output
3. **Multi-model flexibility** ŌĆö switch GPT-5.3, Claude, Gemini within one session
4. **Computer Use agents** ŌĆö visual browser verification of generated UI is a genuine differentiator
5. **Design Mode** ŌĆö annotate browser UI elements directly for agent consumption
6. **IDE familiarity** ŌĆö VS Code fork means zero editor relearning for most devs
7. **Team pricing** ŌĆö $40/user vs Claude Code's $125/user for teams

---

## 6. Notable Limits / Weaknesses

- **Context window degradation** ŌĆö advertises 200K tokens but real usable window is reported at 70KŌĆō120K in practice
- **VS Code lock-in** ŌĆö can't use outside of their fork; architectural decisions increasingly diverge from upstream
- **Cost at scale** ŌĆö token consumption per agent run reported 12ŌĆō80% higher than CLI alternatives; potential 10x price difference vs Claude Code for identical workflows
- **Conversational-only memory model** ŌĆö no durable artifact-based requirements tracking; rules/prompt files are its only persistence mechanism
- **Community tension** ŌĆö Cursor 3's IDE-to-agent pivot alienated a segment of power users who valued tight synchronous editing control
- **Tightening limits** ŌĆö usage caps reported to tighten quarterly

---

## 7. Competitive Position Summary

Cursor leads on **developer UX, tab completions, visual diff review, and GUI-based agent verification**. It competes on breadth of model access and team pricing. Its weakest flanks relative to this workspace's stack are **effective context window size** and **cost per agent run at scale** ŌĆö both relevant for high-volume App Factory workflows.

---

## Acceptance
Brief filed per DEV-62 mandate. Part of six-vendor benchmark sweep.

STEPS:

(2) Per source, collect new or changed items in the window:

(3) Classify each candidate as MCP or non-MCP. MCP candidates include: anything with `modelcontextprotocol` in the URL or topics, anything named `mcp-*` or `*-mcp`, anything that ships a Model Context Protocol server. Everything else is non-MCP (skill / subagent / template).

(4) Evaluate each candidate:

If that skill is not yet available, fall back to the lightweight inline checklist: (a) what protocol surface it exposes, (b) does it duplicate any tool we already have, (c) install footprint (deps / runtime / auth), (d) maintenance signal (last commit, open issues, stars), (e) any obvious security smell (writes to disk, requires broad OAuth scopes, third-party API exfil).

(5) Bucket each candidate into one of four labels and write the report. Post ONE comment on this autopilot's created issue with this exact shape:

Workspace Upgrade Weekly Loop ŌĆö recursive workspace self-improvement. Mode=create_issue. Runs every Sunday 09:00 Asia/Kuwait (GMT+3).

Run the `workspace-upgrade-report` skill against the live workspace and post a single ranked action list as a comment on the issue this autopilot creates. Auto-dispatch the top 3 actions to skill-builder, prompt-optimizer, and memory-curator. Never skip a week ŌĆö a silent skip is itself a finding.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULES:
- Mode is create_issue. Do not create additional issues except the three sub-issues for top-3 dispatch in step (6).
- NEVER mention CEO in any comment ŌĆö CEO is the assignee and re-mentioning would loop (Factory Pulse v3 no-mention rule).

STEPS:

(1) Scope the scan window. Treat `since = now - 7 days` (RFC3339, UTC). Use `--output json` on every CLI call. Continue on single-call failure (log to stdout and skip that signal).

(2) Collect the five input signals:

   (a) retry_logs (last 7 days). Walk every issue updated in the past 7 days and pull metadata. Any `retry_log` key present is a failure signature. Aggregate by skill name / agent / failure_reason.

       - For each issue, `multica issue metadata list <id> --output json` ŌĆö keep only entries with a `retry_log` key.

   (b) Blocked issues ŌĆö every issue currently `status=blocked`:
       - `multica issue list --status blocked --output json`
       - For each: capture id, identifier, title, assignee, age (now - updated_at), and `blocked_reason` metadata.

   (c) Stale in_review (>48h) ŌĆö every issue currently `status=in_review` whose `updated_at` is more than 48h ago:
       - `multica issue list --status in_review --output json`
       - Filter client-side by `updated_at`.

   (d) Agent WIP imbalance ŌĆö for every agent in the workspace, count `in_progress` issues assigned to them. Flag any agent with WIP=0 (cold) or WIPŌēź4 (overloaded). Flag any squad whose total WIP Ōēź7.
       - `multica agent list --output json`
       - `multica issue list --status in_progress --output json` and group by `assignee_id`.

   (e) Skill orphans ŌĆö every skill not assigned to any agent (`agent_count==0`), and every agent with zero skills attached.
       - `multica skill list --output json`
       - For each skill, attempt `multica skill get <id> --output json` (or inspect the skillŌåÆagent join if exposed). If the CLI exposes no join command, infer from agent definitions (`multica agent get <id> --output json` per agent) ŌĆö cap the scan at 30 agents per run.

(3) Score and rank actions. For each candidate upgrade action, score on:
   - Impact (how many issues/agents this would unblock or accelerate; 1ŌĆō5)
   - Effort (1=trivial, 5=multi-day)
   - Confidence (1=speculative, 5=clear pattern with Ōēź3 occurrences)
   Final score = (Impact ├Ś Confidence) / Effort. Surface the Top 5.

(4) Compose the upgrade report comment. Post ONE comment on this autopilot's created issue, using this exact shape so step (6) can parse it:

## Input

- [ ] No paid resource required

## Input
The agentic ecosystem is moving fast (MCP, Anthropic skills, awesome-claude-code-*). We have no rigorous evaluator for new MCP servers, which means we either ignore them or import them blind.

issues.description:
## Input
DEV-43 hit retry_log=3 because no skill captured the proven Android-APK-local-build pattern (Expo prebuild ŌåÆ Gradle assembleRelease with debug keystore, no EAS dependency). The next mobile app should not start from zero.

## Expected Output
Skill `android-apk-local-builder`. Input: Expo project path + env vars + target version. Steps:
1. Install Android SDK 34 + build-tools 34.0.0 + platform-tools via `sdkmanager` if missing (idempotent)
2. `expo prebuild --platform android --clean`
3. Inject env vars into `android/app/build.gradle` (versionCode, versionName, applicationId)
4. Generate / reuse debug keystore (RSA 2048, 10000 validity)
5. `cd android && ./gradlew assembleRelease`
6. Output APK path; verify Ōēż 60 MB; verify `adb install` on Android 24+ emulator

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Idempotent ŌĆö re-run produces the same APK on the same input
- [ ] Self-heal: 2 gradle failures with same error ŌåÆ swap to `npx eas-cli build --local`
- [ ] Documented limitation: debug-signed only; production-signing is a separate skill

## DoD
Skill published. DEV-43's exact recipe folded in. First reuse cuts mobile-build time by Ōēź 50%.
issues.title: SKILL: android-apk-local-builder ŌĆö lift DEV-43's gradle+expo recipe into reusable skill
